title: Scale & reflect absolute value graphs
description: y=a|x| is a stretched/shrunk version of the absolute value function y=|x|. Practice this relationship between the graphical and algebraic representations of absolute value functions.

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is reflected across the $x$-axis and then scaled vertically by a factor of $7$.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Multiplying the output of $|x|$ by a constant $a$ scales the graph vertically.

$y_{\text{transformed}}=a|x|$

If $|a|>1$, the graph is stretched vertically, and if $|a|<1$, the graph is compressed vertically.

When $a<0$, the graph is also reflected across the $x$-axis.

We want to scale by a factor of $7$ so $|a|=7$. This means that the graph is stretched vertically.

Because we also reflect across the $x$-axis, $a=-7$.

The equation of the new graph is:

$y=-7|x|$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is reflected across the $x$-axis and then scaled vertically by a factor of $\dfrac{5}{3}$.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Multiplying the output of $|x|$ by a constant $a$ scales the graph vertically.

$y_{\text{transformed}}=a|x|$

If $|a|>1$, the graph is stretched vertically, and if $|a|<1$, the graph is compressed vertically.

When $a<0$, the graph is also reflected across the $x$-axis.

We want to scale by a factor of $\dfrac{5}{3}$ so $|a|=\dfrac{5}{3}$. This means that the graph is stretched vertically.

Because we also reflect across the $x$-axis, $a=-\dfrac{5}{3}$.

The equation of the new graph is:

$y=-\dfrac{5}{3}|x|$

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $g$ can be thought of as a scaled version of $f(x)=|x|$.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the equation for $g(x)$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation of a scaled transformation of the graph of $f(x)=|x|$ can have the form:

$g(x)=\green{a}|x|$

If $|\green{a}|>1$, the graph is stretched vertically, and if $|\green{a}|<1$, the graph is compressed vertically.

When $\green{a}<0$, the graph is then reflected across the $x$-axis.

Let's compare corresponding points in $\blueD{f}$ and $\maroonD{g}$. For example, $\blueD{f}(4)=4$ and $\maroonD{g}(4)=2$.

$\dfrac{2}{4}=0.5$

Each $y$-coordinate in $\maroonD{g}$ is $0.5$ times the distance from the $x$-axis as the corresponding $y$-coordinate in $\blueD{f}$.  

So, $\greenD{a}=0.5$.

[[☃ interaction 1]]

In conclusion,

$g(x)=0.5|x|$

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes both scale by one. The graph of f is V-shaped with a minimum at zero, zero. On the left side of the minimum is a ray with a slope of negative one, and on the right side is a ray with a slope of one. The graph of g is V-shaped with a minimum at zero, zero. On the left side of the minimum is a ray with a slope of negative one-half, and on the right side is a ray with a slope of one-half.
